title: FedTrajPO++ ‚ÄĒ Federated Trajectory Policy Optimization for Tool-Calling LLM Agents
summary: ECG 618 Final Paper. Compliance-constrained federated trajectory DPO for tool-using LLM agents. FedTrajPO++ reduces violations by 94.6% and tool calls by 74.2% vs. FedAvg-SFT on ToolWorld-v2, via warm-started mixed SFT+DPO, modular skill bank, and support-aware aggregation. Not the raw-success winner ‚ÄĒ a safety-cost trade-off.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 ‚ÄĒ Problem 4: FFNN Two-Input Nonlinear System Modeling
summary: 6-input, 60-neuron FFNN (Levenberg-Marquardt, tansig/purelin) trained to model y(k)=cos(ŌÄx‚āĀ)cos(ŌÄx‚āā)+0.25x‚āĀ¬≤. Max approximation error = 0.000804 ‚Č™ 0.15, R¬≤=1.000000. Neighbourhood input design supplies local curvature context to the network.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 ‚ÄĒ Problem 3: Neural Network Character Recognition (E/C/U/S)
summary: Two-layer FFNN (20‚Üí4‚Üí2, tanh, backprop) trained in 6627 epochs (ő∑=0.2, tolerance=10‚ĀĽ‚Āī) to classify 5√ó4 binary characters E, C, U, S. Seven unknown test letters decoded as SUCCESS. Network tolerates 1-pixel deviations from training templates.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #1 ‚ÄĒ Wiener Solution, Leaky LMS & Nonlinear Adaptive Filter
summary: Three-problem midterm: (1) 2-weight Wiener solution for noisy periodic input, W*=[1,4]^T, convergence range 0<őľ<1/3; (2) Leaky LMS derivation, stability bound őľ<1/(őĪ+őĽ_max), steady-state E[W]=(őĪI+R)^{-1}P; (3) Nonlinear tanh adaptive filter update W_{n+1}=W_n+2őľőĶ(n)[1‚ąíy¬≤(n)]X_n.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 ‚ÄĒ Problem 2: IIR LMS Adaptive Inverse System
summary: IIR LMS identification of the inverse of a 3rd-order IIR plant H_p(z). 5-weight adaptive IIR filter (a0,a1,a2,b1,b2) converges to w_opt=[1,‚ąí1.2,0.6,‚ąí1.3,‚ąí0.8] in ~3.4√ó10¬≥ iterations (őľ=0.001, N=80,000). Cascade H(z)H_p(z)‚Čą1 verified via frequency response (0 dB, 0¬į phase).

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 ‚ÄĒ Problem 1: Adaptive System ID via LMS, SER & RLS
summary: 10-weight adaptive FIR identification of a 3rd-order IIR plant using LMS (őľ=0.0008, M=0.8%, T_MSE‚Čą316 iter.), SER (őĪ=0.995, T_MSE‚Čą320 iter.), and RLS (őĽ=1, T_MSE‚Čą1.65 iter.). Includes Wiener solution, weight trajectories, learning curves, and frequency-response comparisons.

title: ECG 783 HW2 ‚ÄĒ FIR Identification of an IIR System via Wiener Solution
summary: FIR modelling of the IIR system d(k)=0.5d(k-1)+32x(k)+32x(k-1) using the Wiener‚ÄďHopf solution. Compares L+1=2 (MSE‚Čą787) and L+1=10 (MSE‚Čą0.012), with impulse-response truncation analysis and frequency-response comparison.

title: ECG 783 HW1 ‚ÄĒ Wiener Solution for the Adaptive Linear Combiner
summary: Wiener‚ÄďHopf solution for a 2-tap and 10-tap FIR ALC on two signal pairs: sinusoidal input (Case 1) and square-wave input (Case 2). Includes correlation estimation, MSE analysis, and frequency-response interpretation.

title: ECG 783 Adaptive Signal Processing with Neural Network EXAM
summary: Four-problem exam covering 10-weight adaptive combiner (LMS/SER/RLS), IIR LMS inverse system, neural network character recognition (E/C/U/S), and FFNN two-input system modeling.
